title: Do you pay for market data, or is direct exchange API enough?
author: tunedforai
selftext:
Genuine question for people running live bots: do you ever pay for market data, or is direct exchange API + CCXT enough for everything you need?

I aggregate orderflow across 20 exchanges: buy/sell ratios, CVD, funding rates, liquidation pressure, all in one call. Built it because I needed it for my own trading system. Now trying to figure out if anyone else would actually pay for this or if everyone just builds their own.

The raw price/candle data is obviously free everywhere. What I'm trying to understand is whether the derived stuff (cross-exchange flow aggregation, regime detection, who's accumulating vs distributing) has value to anyone, or if everyone doing serious algo trading already computes that themselves.

Genuinely trying to figure out if this is a product or just a personal tool.  Thanks!

comments.author: Otherwise_Wave9374
comments.body: If you can quantify the edge (better fills, fewer false signals, faster regime detection), people will pay, but the bar is high because serious folks either build it or distrust black boxes. What usually sells is: clean API docs, latency guarantees, and a free tier with limited symbols/timeframes so they can validate. Also, derived metrics need clear definitions or youll get nonstop support questions. For positioning/packaging ideas, Ive seen a few good patterns discussed here: https://blog.promarkia.com/
